pages.raw_text:
Secretary Ballinger ran away
yesterday afternoon. He would not
stay at the convention hall and al
low the delegates the privilege of
questioning him-according to the
rules Of the congress and a privi-
lege that every other speaker in
the history Of the congress has
granted the delegates. When
George Chamberlain, now senator
from Oregon, quizzed Hartman at
the irrigation congress at Sacra-
mento two years ago, Hartman
stayed and had it out like a fighter.


But Ballinger fastened away in
his automobile to his hotel, where
he ensconced himself behind his
private secretary.


1t came about in this wise: After
Ballinger has read his carefully
prepared speech, to which no one
could take any exception, John
Fairweather, delegate from Califor-
